description: DejaViewed catalog — structured knowledge index. Query by tier, domain, type, tool, technique, or full-text search across titles and summaries.

usage.programmatic: Fetch this JSON, filter entries[] by any field. Indices map keywords → entry IDs for fast lookup.
usage.agent_context: Load context.md alongside this file for AI agent reasoning about available knowledge.
usage.web: Visit https://dejaviewed.dev for the interactive catalog.

entries.title: L1B3RT4S — Pliny jailbreak prompts still landing on GPT-5
entries.summary: @_dbrogle tests elder-plinius's L1B3RT4S jailbreak repo (github.com/elder-plinius/L1B3RT4S) against GPT-5 and it still works despite tighter safety. Worth revisiting for red-team prompt libraries.
entries.creator: @_dbrogle
takeaways: Bookmark L1B3RT4S as a jailbreak corpus for red-team testing
Useful for hardening Victor's own agents against injection

entries.title: Platonic Representation Hypothesis — embeddings as AI telepathy
entries.summary: @lizthedeveloper riffs on the Platonic Representation Hypothesis paper, framing shared embedding spaces across models as a kind of 'AI telepathy'. Pointer back to the original research paper on vector/embedding convergence.
entries.creator: @lizthedeveloper
takeaways: Platonic Representation paper implies cross-model embedding translation is viable

entries.title: OpenAI GPT-5 prompting framework — clarity, context, role
entries.summary: @theaifield summarizes OpenAI's own GPT-5 prompting guide: be explicit with instructions, load context heavily, assign a role. Carousel-style breakdown; actual value is remembering OpenAI published an official prompt guide.
entries.creator: @theaifield
takeaways: Pull OpenAI's official GPT-5 prompting guide as a reference doc

entries.title: GeoSpy.ai — geolocate any photo from buildings, plants, shadows
entries.summary: GeoSpy.ai infers photo location from architectural, botanical and lighting cues against a geotagged image database. Link: https://geospy.ai/ . OSINT/geolocation tool worth bookmarking.
entries.creator: @innovation
takeaways: Useful OSINT primitive
Test against Victor's own photos to understand leak surface

entries.title: Build Wars recap — AI gets you 80%, designers finish the 20%
entries.summary: @itsdesignertom's newsletter dissecting the Brotzky $100 / Jhey CSS Build Wars showdown. Core lesson: AI nails the first 80% of a UI but handcrafted tweaks still matter. Published via @uxtoolsco.
entries.creator: @itsdesignertom
takeaways: Treat vibe-coded UI as 80% scaffolding, always hand-tune last 20%

entries.title: Gemini CLI — free terminal coding agent, 1K req/day, Gemini 2.5 Pro
entries.summary: Google's Gemini CLI: free AI coding agent in the terminal, 1,000 req/day, 1M context on Gemini 2.5 Pro, plus Veo/Imagen generation. Positioned as a Cursor/Claude Code killer.
entries.creator: @davi.d_roberts
takeaways: Install Gemini CLI as a free fallback coding agent
1000 reqs/day free tier

entries.title: JSON image-prompt profiles — parameterized context for ChatGPT
entries.summary: @chase.variantai demos using structured JSON 'context profiles' to drive image generation in ChatGPT, with dynamically swappable parameters. Proof-of-concept technique for repeatable AI image prompts.
entries.creator: @chase.variantai

entries.title: Vibe-coded games era — voxel MMO in a day, Claude plays Pokemon
entries.summary: @itsdesignertom riff on the AI-game-dev moment: a multiplayer voxel game shipped in a day, a banana-clicker sitting at Steam #6, Claude playing an AI-generated Pokemon clone. Cultural snapshot of indie-AI gamedev vs big studios.
entries.creator: @itsdesignertom

entries.title: Blender MCP + Tripo — 2D reference image to full 3D scene
entries.summary: @bilawal.ai demos a Blender MCP integration that takes a 2D reference image, has an LLM decompose the scene, generates 3D assets via Tripo, and arranges them to match. Forward-looking on Unity/Unreal MCP equivalents.
entries.creator: @bilawal.ai
takeaways: Blender MCP + Tripo = auto 3D scene assembly from reference image
Extend to Unity/Unreal next

entries.title: MediaPipe TouchDesigner plugin by @blankensmithing
entries.summary: @neles0n trying the MediaPipe plugin by @blankensmithing inside TouchDesigner. Useful pointer for anyone doing body/hand tracking in TD creative-coding pipelines.
entries.creator: @neles0n

entries.title: AI rotoscoping — mask yourself into your own animations
entries.summary: @iamfesq demos using an AI detection/segmentation model to auto-rotoscope a mask of himself for compositing into his animated art. Quick technique reference for AI-assisted rotoscoping.
entries.creator: unknown

entries.title: Ollama — run open-source LLMs locally to learn how they work
entries.summary: @edhonour: short PSA that installing Ollama is the easiest way to run open-source models locally and poke at their internals. Baseline local-LLM entry point.
entries.creator: @edhonour

entries.title: Qwen 2.5 Max, Wan 2.1, Lumina 2 — China's open-source trio
entries.summary: @nick_saraev walks through three open-source Chinese AI models: Qwen 2.5 Max (chat/code/search/image/video), Wan 2.1 (open-source video gen), and Lumina 2 (photoreal image gen). All free, all downloadable.
entries.creator: @nick_saraev

entries.title: Hunyuan3D-2 — Tencent's free image-to-3D model generator
entries.summary: Tencent's Hunyuan3D-2 turns text or an uploaded image into textured 3D models in seconds. Free web UI; pipeline is upload > Generate Shape > Generate Textured Shape > download. Good for game/app asset workflows.
entries.creator: @nick_saraev
takeaways: Pair with HY-Motion for full text->3D->motion pipeline

entries.title: Google's comprehensive prompt engineering guide — PDF recap
entries.summary: @aitoolhub.co reshares @robonuggets's recap of Google's own prompt engineering guide, pitched as the most comprehensive from a major lab. Pointer to the Google whitepaper.

entries.title: Music AI agent experiment — @robonuggets
entries.summary: Short clip of a music-composition AI agent experiment reshared by @aitoolhub.co, originally from @robonuggets on TikTok. Reference point for music-agent demos; thin on specifics.
entries.creator: @aitoolhub.co

entries.title: Odyssey Explorer — turn any image into an explorable 3D world
entries.summary: @mattfarmerai on Odyssey Explorer: single image -> navigable 3D world. Relevant for AI world-gen / virtual production workflows.

entries.title: 50-neuron MNIST net — visualized prediction mapping (Eric Vyacheslav)
entries.summary: Visualization (credit Eric Vyacheslav) of a 2-layer, 50-neuron network predicting handwritten digits, showing live activation/prediction mapping. Nice teaching/visual reference.
entries.creator: @beautiful.digits

entries.title: Kling Motion Brush — direct up to 5 characters in one AI video
entries.summary: @realrileybrown on Kling's Motion Brush: select up to 5 characters/objects in a starting image (often Midjourney-generated) and draw motion paths to animate them. One of the sharpest controllable video-gen workflows at the time.
entries.creator: @realrileybrown

entries.title: Runway non-linear video — node canvas of AI keyframes
entries.summary: @realrileybrown explains Runway's 'non-linear AI video exploration': work on a canvas of node-connected keyframes, let AI interpolate between them, branch alternate universes instead of a linear timeline.

entries.title: World Labs — explorable 3D worlds from a single 2D image
entries.summary: @rpn covers World Labs' first demo: generate navigable 3D worlds from one image or sentence, with camera controls and style effects. Referenced as a likely Quest/Vision Pro use case.
entries.creator: @rpn
takeaways: Potential Quest/Vision Pro worldbuilding app

entries.title: Nemotron 70B — NVIDIA's open-source LLM benchmark leader
entries.summary: @mattfarmerai flagging NVIDIA's Nemotron 70B as (at the time) the best open-source LLM. One-line pointer, no deeper analysis.

entries.title: Runway Gen-3 Alpha Video-to-Video — precise motion control
entries.summary: @runwayapp's Gen-3 Alpha Video-to-Video release, introduced as a new control mechanism for driving movement and expression in generations. Original post by @ailevels; rest of caption is AI-field boilerplate.

entries.title: Claude 3.5 Sonnet — build an app in minutes (Shawn Kanungo demo)
entries.summary: @ainewscentral reshare of @shawnkanungo's viral 'build an app in minutes with Claude 3.5' demo. Early Claude vibe-coding showcase.

entries.title: 5 ChatGPT learning prompts — 80/20, intern mode, skill plan

entries.summary: @edward.builds SEO tactic: in Google Search Console, filter for keywords ranking at positions 6-11, add a few sentences of relevant copy to the page, often jumps to page 1. Concrete, low-effort SEO play.

entries.summary: @rajistics repost flagging TensorBoard's embedding projector as a quick way to visualize high-dimensional embeddings in 2D/3D. One-liner pointer.

entries.summary: @sebastian_volkis built and shipped BopIt.app in 72 hours using Claude 3.5 Sonnet, pulling 42K views and hundreds of users in 12 hours. Early case study of solo Claude-built launches.

entries.title: Scraping SAM.gov — puppeteer + AI for government data
entries.summary: @itsmikepowers on scraping US government (SAM.gov-style) data with Puppeteer and AI automation. Thin on specifics but worth remembering for gov-contract data workflows.

entries.summary: @codewithclaude pitches blackbox.ai as his classroom coding assistant to bypass no-ChatGPT rules. Reference point for the Blackbox AI coding tool.

entries.title: Sketch-to-3D-anim pipeline — Procreate + Magnific + 3DAI + Move AI
entries.summary: Director Jon Finger's pipeline: Procreate sketches -> Magnific AI style transfer -> 3DAI Studio for 3D models -> Move AI for mocap -> Cinema 4D for render. Concrete multi-tool AI animation workflow.

entries.title: Boris Cherny's Claude Code workflow — parallel worktrees + plan mode
entries.summary: @evolving.ai summarizes Boris Cherny's X thread on how the Anthropic team actually uses Claude Code: 3-5 git worktrees in parallel, start every complex task in plan mode, update rules after every mistake, build reusable skills and subagents.
entries.creator: @evolving.ai
takeaways: Adopt 3-5 parallel worktrees per feature as default Claude Code workflow
This matches Victor's dispatching-parallel-agents skill

entries.title: Bash reference manual mirror — unexpected location easter egg
entries.summary: @yousuckatprogramming flags that an alternative mirror of the bash reference manual shows up in an unexpected place. Meme-ish but useful bash-doc pointer.
entries.creator: @yousuckatprogramming

entries.title: Anthropic blocks Claude Max subscription tokens in 3rd-party tools
entries.summary: @tech.unicorn on Anthropic killing subscription-token access in third-party tools (Jan 9 ToS change), spiking effective cost from $200/mo to $500-$2000/mo on API. Suggests multi-model routers (DeepSeek/Gemini/Llama/GPT-4o) as the mitigation.
entries.creator: @tech.unicorn
takeaways: Subscription-token automation is TOS-risky; use API keys for production agents

entries.title: DATA CONTROL — Kinect + TouchDesigner + Unreal scientific viz
entries.summary: @pala3d's 'DATA CONTROL' project: real-time visualization of NASA/CERN datasets as particle systems, body-controlled via Kinect through TouchDesigner into Unreal Engine. Pipeline explicitly human-made, no AI. Reference for data-viz/creative-coding stack.
entries.creator: @pala3d

entries.title: Boris Cherny's personal CLAUDE.md — plan-first, self-improving rules
entries.summary: @keshavsuki dissects Boris Cherny's (Anthropic) personal CLAUDE.md: plan-first, subagents for complex work, self-improving lessons.md loop, hard verification standard, elegance demand, autonomous bug fixing, git discipline. High-signal template for CLAUDE.md design.
entries.creator: @keshavsuki

entries.title: Claude Code dashboard — have Claude set up its own status UI
entries.summary: @toddybuilds tip: if Claude feels overwhelming, ask Claude to generate a dashboard that summarizes what it's doing. Light but useful meta-prompt idea.
entries.creator: @toddybuilds

entries.title: Open-source geopolitics terminal — OSINT feeds + AI instability scores
entries.summary: @brahada_irl teasing a newly open-sourced 'Bloomberg Terminal for geopolitics': real-time global map with military flights/ships, OSINT news scanning across 100+ sources, automated instability scores, dark-ship detection, prediction-market signals. Comment-gated link.
entries.creator: @brahada_irl
takeaways: Adjacent to Horus-Eye (ai4#56) — both are AI OSINT dashboards

entries.title: primer.md + memory.sh — live-updating CLAUDE.md companion
entries.summary: @keshavsuki on fixing CLAUDE.md staleness: add a primer.md that Claude rewrites at end of every session (current state, next step, blockers), plus a memory.sh launch hook that injects last 5 commits and modified files, plus a git hook logging commits. Four-layer always-current context.
entries.creator: @keshavsuki
takeaways: Add primer.md + end-of-session rewrite rule to Victor's Claude Code projects

entries.title: Claude Code + NotebookLM integration setup
entries.summary: @stevearnesonofficial teaser: setup guide for wiring Claude Code into NotebookLM. Comment-gated, content itself thin — note only.

entries.title: Greg Isenberg — how to design stunning AI apps (not vibe-coded ugly)
entries.summary: @gregisenberg teaser on designing polished AI apps rather than the 'vibe-coded slop' default. Caption is thin; content lives in the video.

entries.title: CLAUDE.md explained — vibe-coding primer
entries.summary: @soojintech short explainer on what CLAUDE.md is and why it '10x's work in Claude Code. Intro-level reference.

entries.summary: @jens.heitmann: three Claude Code 'systems' to unlock design quality in CC projects. Teaser for the techniques; useful as a pointer to Jens's CC design content.

entries.title: Claude Code + Chrome — browser-driven coding combo
entries.summary: @casey.aicreates flags the combination of Claude Code and Chrome (likely Chrome DevTools MCP or browser automation) as a big time-saver. Caption is thin; the pointer is the combo itself.

entries.title: Claude Opus 4.6 — McKinsey-style research prompts (TAM/SWOT/CAC)
entries.summary: @evolving.ai on using Opus 4.6 as a structured consultant: prompt templates for TAM sizing, competitive landscape, unit economics (CAC/LTV), combined SWOT/risk briefs. Framed as 'replaces $5K consultant work'.

entries.title: 7 must-install Claude Code repos — Obsidian Skills, n8n-MCP, Superpowers
entries.summary: @chase.h.ai lists 7 Claude Code repos with GitHub links: kepano/obsidian-skills, czlonkowski/n8n-mcp, nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill, thedotmack/claude-mem, glittercowboy/get-shit-done, hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code, obra/superpowers. High-signal resource dump.

Install obsidian-skills for vault-as-second-brain

entries.title: Boris Cherny — 10-15 parallel Claude sessions + evolving CLAUDE.md
entries.summary: @mindwired.ai summary of Boris Cherny's workflow: 10-15 Claude sessions in parallel across terminal and web, CLAUDE.md continuously updated after mistakes as reusable guardrails, Claude-CLI for data queries. Notes Claude Code's share of public GitHub activity.
entries.creator: @mindwired.ai

entries.title: Polymarket weather edge — dorm weather station, $100K+ traded
entries.summary: @insidesteal profile of a student generating $100K+ on Polymarket weather contracts using a dorm-built weather station and localized data asymmetry. Reference for prediction-market quant plays built on proprietary data.
entries.creator: @insidesteal
takeaways: Real edge on Polymarket = exclusive real-time data source, not better ML

entries.title: Claude Code rate-limit workarounds — Nate Herkai's tips
entries.summary: @nateherkai teaser for a YouTube breakdown of his best tips for hitting and managing Claude Code usage limits. Real content lives on YouTube.
entries.creator: @nateherkai

entries.title: Turboquant — memory backend for Claude/Open Claw AI stores
entries.summary: @eddynthings quick plug for Turboquant as a memory store helping his AI agent remember more. Thin on specifics but worth noting Turboquant as a memory tool.
entries.creator: @eddynthings

entries.title: Polymarket 99c limit-order edge — 75 automated trades/hour
entries.summary: @beinginvested on a Polymarket trader placing limit orders on outcomes already closing at 99c, automated at 75 trades/hour, 4 months to crack it. Teaser with DM-gated full breakdown; worth remembering the pattern.
entries.creator: @beinginvested
takeaways: Test late-contract 99c limit harvesting as a low-variance income strategy

entries.title: llms.txt — get ChatGPT to recommend your local business
entries.summary: @brycenwood.ai got ChatGPT to recommend his vehicle-wrap shop via an llms.txt file (built in 5min with Claude Code) describing business, location, value prop. Early GEO (generative engine optimization) tactic.
entries.creator: @brycenwood.ai
takeaways: Ship llms.txt on every Victor project to become LLM-discoverable

entries.title: Shepherd dog flock simulation — pressure-field + boids rules
entries.summary: @simulateitnow explains a flock-herding simulation combining a pressure-field force F=(1-d/R)^1.2 with classic boids (cohesion/alignment/separation). Great reference for swarm/multi-agent simulation math.
entries.creator: @simulateitnow

entries.title: Custom cloud memory/'brains' for LLMs — Angus Sewell take
entries.summary: @angus.sewell argues that most people are still running local memory stacks and that custom cloud-based LLM 'brains' are about to become a big category. Directional take, no specific tool.
entries.creator: @angus.sewell

entries.title: Quant alpha discovery primer — Kalman filters, stat arb, XGBoost
entries.summary: @sorhan.hq profile of Guangting Yu (applied math PhD, published NeurIPS). Lists the alpha-hunting toolkit: statistical arbitrage, Fama-French factors, Kalman filters, mean reversion, momentum, XGBoost/NNs. Plus covariance formula. Good intro-level quant reference.
entries.creator: @sorhan.hq

entries.title: Ornstein-Uhlenbeck mean-reversion visualized — theta/mu/sigma
entries.summary: @quant.traderr visualization of the OU process for mean reversion (vol, rates, pairs-trade spreads) with a probability cloud and live path. DM-gated Python repo for tuning theta/mu/sigma.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr

entries.title: Kimi K2 — keeps up with Claude on web app generation
entries.summary: @lizthedeveloper: quick test confirming Moonshot's Kimi K2 is competitive with Claude for building web apps. Signal that Kimi K2 is worth adding to the coding-model rotation.
entries.creator: @lizthedeveloper

entries.title: Onyx — open-source self-hosted agentic RAG platform
entries.summary: @marc.kaz flags onyx-dot-app/onyx hitting #1 GitHub trending: self-hostable AI platform with agentic RAG, deep research mode, custom agents, web+code execution, voice/image gen, 50+ connectors. Strong self-hosted AI stack option.
entries.creator: @marc.kaz
takeaways: Clone and evaluate as self-hosted Claude/ChatGPT replacement
50+ connectors = instant data-source integration

entries.title: PromptOps Smart Action Detector — save tokens, run commands locally
entries.summary: @shellonback on PromptOps' new detector: when a sub-agent is about to run a directly-executable command (npm run deploy/build/test), it suggests running in the terminal instead of sending to the LLM. Concrete anti-token-waste pattern.
entries.creator: @shellonback
takeaways: Hard rule: never ask an LLM to run a command you could bash yourself

entries.title: Claude 'caveman' mode — token-saving terse-output skill
entries.summary: @sourcetms made a 'caveman' Claude skill (from a Reddit post) that forces terse caveman-speak responses to save tokens. Meme-y but a legit compression technique for long sessions.
entries.creator: @sourcetms

entries.title: Ant Colony Optimization sim — pheromone feedback + diffusion PDE
entries.summary: @simulateitnow explains ACO / stigmergy with the diffusion-evaporation equation ∂τ/∂t = Δτ + D∇²τ - ρτ, showing how short paths win through faster pheromone reinforcement. Great visual reference for swarm optimization.
entries.creator: @simulateitnow

entries.title: NASA EONET — free real-time weather/event data API
entries.summary: @softwarewithnick points at NASA's free real-time natural events data feed (EONET-style): wildfires, earthquakes, storms via satellite. Useful open data source for mapping/alerting projects.
entries.creator: @softwarewithnick
takeaways: Free real-time disaster/weather feed — direct input for prediction market strategies

entries.title: 5 must-install Claude MCP servers — @raycfu list
entries.summary: @raycfu short list of 5 MCP servers worth installing in Claude. Caption is thin; value is using it as an MCP pointer to revisit in the video.
entries.creator: @raycfu

entries.title: Crypto dollar-neutral reversal — naive long/short, eaten by fees
entries.summary: @avi_mukesh: simple reversal strategy on crypto (short if up, long if down), dollar-neutral scaling. Looks good without costs, transaction fees kill it. Honest starter example for learning backtesting caveats.
entries.creator: @avi_mukesh

entries.title: Claude Code 'brevity mandate' — leaked system prompt tradeoff
entries.summary: @gojutechtalk on the leaked Claude Code system prompt's 'brevity mandate' rule that pushes CC to minimize code changes, which he argues leads to spaghetti-on-spaghetti fixes. Useful critique to remember.
entries.creator: @gojutechtalk
takeaways: Override brevity mandate in CLAUDE.md for code-quality critical projects

entries.title: STE.GG — open-source AI steganography toolkit (hide in anything)
entries.summary: @marc.kaz on STE.GG: open-source steganography platform that hides payloads in images, audio, text, PDFs, packets, ZIPs, emojis. Built-in AI agent auto-tries 120+ LSB combinations, DCT, PVD, chroma, palette, etc. Both reveal and conceal modes.

entries.title: AlgoExchange — 7-signal Nasdaq futures intraday momentum system
entries.summary: @algoexchange walks through an intraday momentum system on Nasdaq futures: 7 independent momentum signals combined via a normalized linear risk function, exposure scaling with confirmations. Claims $35M+ live capital across variants. Commercial but mathematically explicit.

entries.title: Polymarket insider detection — websocket + data-feed signals
entries.summary: @carsoncantcode on using Polymarket websockets and data feeds to identify insider-trade patterns (referencing a recent arrest). Technique pointer for prediction-market forensics.

entries.summary: @quantguild short pointing to their YouTube rundown 'The 5 Papers that Built Modern Quant Finance'. Pointer to the QG video and Discord for canonical quant reading list.

entries.title: Edge-of-quant papers — relativity, fractals, biology in markets
entries.summary: @mirkovicdev teases a list of unusual quant finance papers: relativity in option pricing, fractal/chaos vol modeling, biology affecting trading performance. DM-gated full list.

entries.title: Linux inside a 6MB PDF — RISC-V emulator embedded in a PDF
entries.summary: @marc.kaz on a demo embedding a RISC-V emulator running Linux inside a standard 6MB PDF, runnable in a PDF reader. Cursed but real; good demo of PDF JS runtime abuse.

entries.title: What elite quant funds actually hire for — 5 traits
entries.summary: @heidioftheeast lists what top funds screen for: obsessive original GitHub work, Math Olympiad / competition medals (~45% of hires), public-failure resilience, self-directed problem solving, being memorable in interview. High-signal hiring lens.
entries.creator: @heidioftheeast

entries.title: 5 starter quant research papers — Value/Momentum, order book, RL
entries.summary: @heidioftheeast: 5 papers to start with — 'Value and Momentum Everywhere', cross-validated covariance estimation, deep-learning sizing on Eurodollar futures, 'The Price Impact of Order Book Events', AI-driven OTC liquidity provision. Concrete reading list.
entries.creator: @heidioftheeast

entries.title: 10 non-cliche quant research projects — Bayesian BL, RL factors, PBO
entries.summary: @heidioftheeast's 10 quant project ideas beyond Black-Scholes: probability of backtest overfitting, Bayesian Black-Litterman, survival-based risk, RL factor portfolios, Sharpe statistics, convex portfolio trading, TS vs XS factors, numeraire-invariant hedging, exotic option model risk, regime-switching factors.
entries.creator: @heidioftheeast

entries.title: 10 institutional quant projects — Avellaneda-Stoikov, Hawkes, HMM
entries.summary: @heidioftheeast's deeper 10-project list: Avellaneda-Stoikov market maker, multivariate Hawkes order-flow, Almgren-Chriss cross-impact, eigenportfolios via random matrix theory, realized-kernel vol forecasting, meta-labeling, orthogonal alpha stacking, HMM regime detection, adversarial stress tests, capacity/impact scaling. Serious portfolio builder.
entries.creator: @heidioftheeast

entries.title: GitReverse — paste any repo URL, get the prompt that built it
entries.summary: @marc.kaz on GitReverse.com: paste a GitHub link and it reverse-engineers the original prompt that would have produced the repo. Useful for prompt archaeology on vibe-coded projects.
entries.creator: @marc.kaz

entries.title: Codex + Claude Code — 3 systems to audit CC output with Codex

entries.title: Ising Model — modeling herd behavior and market phase transitions
entries.summary: 3D lattice visualization of the Ising model applied to financial markets. Blue=sellers, orange=buyers. At high volatility the market is random; as social pressure increases, clusters form leading to spontaneous symmetry breaking (crashes/trends). Built in Python for agent-based modeling.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Ising model maps statistical mechanics to market herd behavior
Phase transitions explain sudden market regime changes
DM-bait for code repo

entries.title: Phase Space Reconstruction — unfolding hidden BTC volatility dynamics
entries.summary: Uses Phase Space Reconstruction (PSR) with time delay tau=3 to unfold BTCUSD volatility into a multi-dimensional manifold. Reveals whether market is in stable regime or chaotic transition. Key for quantitative regime detection.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: PSR reveals hidden market dynamics beyond price vs time
Clustered attractors suggest predictability
Scattered points indicate chaotic regimes

entries.title: Heston Model — stochastic volatility for realistic options pricing
entries.summary: Explains why Black-Scholes fails (constant vol assumption) and how the Heston Model introduces stochastic volatility. Captures the volatility smile and skew that basic models miss. Essential for options pricing.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Black-Scholes assumes constant vol — unrealistic
Heston models vol as its own random process
Captures smile and skew for better options pricing

entries.title: Cox Process — doubly stochastic Poisson for bursty market events
entries.summary: Standard Poisson processes assume constant event rates, but markets are bursty. The Cox Process (Doubly Stochastic Poisson) uses a CIR-driven random intensity to model trade arrivals, defaults, and volatility spikes more realistically.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Markets have bursty event arrivals, not constant rates
Cox Process models random intensity
Applications: order book, defaults, vol spikes

entries.title: Hidden Markov Models — detecting market regime shifts automatically
entries.summary: Uses HMMs to mathematically deduce hidden market states from observable returns and volatility. Identifies low-vol bull, high-vol bear, and recovery regimes. Better than lagging indicators for strategy selection.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: HMMs detect hidden market states from observable data
Match strategy to regime: trend in bull, mean-revert in range
Superior to lagging indicator-based regime detection

entries.title: Avellaneda-Stoikov — optimal spread calculation for market makers
entries.summary: The Avellaneda-Stoikov model solves the stochastic control problem for market making. Calculates optimal bid-ask spread based on volatility, time horizon, and inventory. Foundation of modern HFT market making. DM-bait for code repo.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Calculates optimal spread, not price direction
Balances inventory risk vs profit
Foundation of modern algorithmic market making

entries.title: Ornstein-Uhlenbeck Process — mean reversion for pairs trading
entries.summary: Simulates the OU process showing mean reversion dynamics with theta (reversion speed), mu (equilibrium), and sigma (noise). Core model for pairs trading, interest rate spreads, and volatility signals in quantitative finance.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: OU process models mean-reverting behavior
Key params: theta (speed), mu (mean), sigma (noise)
Applied to pairs trading and rate spreads

entries.title: Wave Function Collapse analogy — modeling pre-event price uncertainty
entries.summary: Draws analogy between quantum wave function collapse and price discovery after news events. Pre-event price is a probability distribution; the event collapses it to a single reality. Emphasizes modeling uncertainty and tail risks for systematic trading.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Pre-event prices represent probability distributions
News collapses uncertainty into price discovery
Model tail risks, don't ignore them

entries.title: 3D Yield Curve Surface — visualizing cross-asset macro regimes
entries.summary: 3D visualization of yield curve dynamics across macro regimes: COVID-19 vol shock (VIX spike, yield compression), Fed tightening cycle (curve inversion). Shows how Treasury yield warping drives cross-asset correlations.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Yield curve shape drives cross-asset pricing
Vol shocks compress yields as capital flees to safety
Tightening cycles invert the curve

entries.title: Volatility Surface — regime shifts from smile to smirk
entries.summary: 3D visualization of implied volatility surface (moneyness x maturity x IV). Shows how the surface warps from gentle 'smile' to steep 'smirk' before tail-risk events as markets bid up OTM puts for downside protection.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Vol surface shape reveals market fear
Smile → smirk transition signals tail risk
OTM put demand warps the surface before crashes

entries.title: Omori's Law — earthquake aftershock math applied to market crashes
entries.summary: Applies geophysics' Omori's Law to financial markets. Post-crash volatility aftershocks decay at a predictable rate following power-law dynamics, not randomly. Shows March 2020 crash decay pattern. DM-bait for code repo.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Post-crash vol decays by power law, not randomly
Same math as earthquake aftershock prediction
Can time post-crash mean reversion entries

entries.title: Wasserstein Distance — measuring the backtest-to-live reality gap
entries.summary: Uses Wasserstein Distance and Sinkhorn Divergence to mathematically measure drift between backtest distributions (blue) and live market conditions (orange). Detects distribution shift that kills more strategies than bad fees.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: High backtest Sharpe doesn't guarantee live performance
Wasserstein distance quantifies strategy drift
Monitor distribution shift to kill dead strategies early

entries.title: Singular Spectrum Analysis — non-parametric trend extraction from noisy data
entries.summary: SSA decomposes time-series into trend, cycles, and noise without assuming a fixed functional form. Breaks data into reconstructed components (RC 1+7 trend, RC 2+3 annual, RC 9 high-freq). DM-bait for code repo.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: SSA extracts trends without assuming functional form
Separates signal components by eigenvalue decomposition
Robust alternative to Fourier for non-stationary data

entries.title: Lempel-Ziv Complexity — compression algorithms to detect market predictability
entries.summary: Applies the LZ compression algorithm (behind .zip files) to financial time series. When LZ complexity is low, patterns repeat and the market is more predictable. When high, it's random noise. Novel cross-domain application.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Low LZ complexity = repeating patterns = more predictable
High LZ complexity = random noise = less tradeable
Cross-domain: compression math applied to markets

entries.title: Fast Fourier Transform — decomposing NVDA price into frequency signals
entries.summary: Applies FFT to NVDA price action to decompose it into frequency components, separating noise from dominant cycles. Shows how to identify the strongest periodic signals in price data.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: FFT separates noise from dominant market cycles
Identifies strongest periodic signals in price data
Foundation for frequency-based trading strategies

entries.title: Monte Carlo — 25,000 shuffled BTC returns to separate edge from luck
entries.summary: Runs 25,000 Monte Carlo simulations by shuffling daily BTC-USD returns to test whether a strategy's performance is genuine edge or statistical luck. One backtest isn't enough — real quants demand statistical significance.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: One backtest proves nothing — could be luck
25K permutations reveal true edge distribution
If random beats your strategy, you have no edge

entries.title: Minimum Spanning Tree — graph theory reveals S&P 500 hidden structure
entries.summary: Applies graph theory (Minimum Spanning Tree) to the S&P 500 to map the hidden network structure between stocks. Reveals sector clusters and systemic risk connections that traditional correlation matrices obscure. DM-bait for code.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: MST reveals hidden market network structure
Sector clusters visible in graph topology
Systemic risk propagates along graph edges

entries.title: Avalanche Model — self-organized criticality explains market crashes
entries.summary: Markets reach self-organized criticality where small perturbations trigger cascading failures. The 'news' doesn't cause the crash — the system was already at a critical state. Applies sandpile/avalanche dynamics from physics to explain market fragility.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Crashes aren't caused by news — system was already critical
Self-organized criticality: small triggers → large cascades
Monitor criticality metrics, not news headlines

entries.title: Tick Data vs OHLC — you're trading on 0.002% of available data
entries.summary: Daily OHLC candles compress ~86,400 seconds of market data into 4 numbers — throwing away 99.998% of information. Makes the case for tick-level and limit order book analysis over candle-based trading.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: OHLC discards 99.998% of market data
Tick-level analysis reveals hidden microstructure
Candle-based trading is information-poor

entries.title: Lyapunov Exponent — measuring chaos in BTC tick-level microstructure
entries.summary: Visualizes the Lyapunov Exponent of BTC-USD microstructure data. Positive exponent = chaotic/unpredictable, negative = deterministic. Reveals that what looks random on 5-min charts has measurable chaotic structure at tick level.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Lyapunov exponent quantifies market chaos
Positive = chaotic divergence, negative = convergence
Tick-level reveals structure hidden in candle charts

entries.title: Kalman Filter — adaptive signal extraction for non-stationary markets
entries.summary: Financial data is non-linear and non-stationary, breaking standard filters. Kalman Filter adapts in real-time, unlike lagging moving averages or FFT (which assumes stationarity). Optimal for real-time signal extraction.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: MAs lag, FFT assumes stationarity — both fail on live data
Kalman Filter adapts to changing market dynamics
Optimal for real-time signal extraction

entries.title: Continuous Wavelet Transform — time-frequency analysis of QQQ
entries.summary: Applies CWT to QQQ to analyze frequency-domain dynamics over time. Unlike FFT (fixed window), wavelets capture how dominant frequencies shift across regimes. Reveals the market's changing 'heartbeat'.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: CWT captures how frequencies shift over time
Superior to FFT for non-stationary data
Reveals regime-dependent dominant cycles

entries.title: Fisher Transform — Gaussian normalization for sharper trading signals
entries.summary: The Fisher Transform mathematically transforms price data into a Gaussian distribution, creating sharper turning-point signals than lagging indicators. Shows clear zero-crossings for entry/exit timing.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Fisher Transform creates near-Gaussian signal distribution
Sharper turning points than moving averages
Clear zero-crossing signals for entries/exits

entries.title: Random Matrix Theory — cleaning correlation matrices from noise
entries.summary: Standard correlation matrices treat random noise as valid connections. Random Matrix Theory (RMT) filters eigenvalues to 'clean' the matrix, keeping only statistically significant correlations. The market is 90% noise.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Most correlations in raw matrices are noise
RMT filters eigenvalues against theoretical noise bounds
Clean matrices improve portfolio optimization

entries.title: Stable Distribution — modeling fat-tailed crashes beyond Gaussian
entries.summary: Markets go up slowly but crash fast. Standard Gaussian models underestimate crash probability. Uses Stable (Levy) distributions to properly model fat tail events and Black Swan risks that beginners ignore.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Gaussian models massively underestimate crash probability
Stable distributions capture fat tails
Ignoring tail risk is the #1 beginner quant mistake

entries.title: Hurst Exponent — 3D surface of BTC memory and mean-reversion zones
entries.summary: Plots the Hurst Exponent of BTC-USD as a 3D surface. H>0.5 = trending (momentum), H<0.5 = mean-reverting, H=0.5 = random walk. Shows how market 'memory' varies across timeframes. Basis for adaptive strategy selection.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: Hurst > 0.5 = trending, < 0.5 = mean-reverting
Market memory varies across timeframes
Use Hurst to select momentum vs mean-reversion strategy

entries.title: VectorBT — vectorized backtesting framework explained simply
entries.summary: Brief explainer of VectorBT, a Python backtesting library that uses vectorized operations for fast strategy testing. Aimed at retail traders transitioning to quantitative methods.
entries.creator: @quant.traderr
takeaways: VectorBT enables fast vectorized strategy backtesting
More accessible entry point for retail → quant transition
